strategy: Attack with Ice (+10%); he resists Fire 40%.

Exploit his self-trap: every 3 rounds he casts a Shadow Meteor Strike (1,650-3,300) and drops a +200% universal Trap on himself. That trap is yours to use, so line up blades and hit into it right after for massive damage.
His Meteor Strike hurts, so soften it with Weakness-type spells and keep shields up on the 3-round timer.
He strips all traps off himself every 2nd round (round 4, 6, and so on), so protect your own traps with Indemnity or rely on the +200% self-trap instead.
Do not Feint him normally: he Swap Wards the trap onto you (Melt or Indemnity avoids it), though Mass Feint and Balance Feint do not trigger it.
Keep your accuracy up since every fizzle gives him 2 power pips. Set up blades, wait for his +200% self-trap, and burst.
cheatNotes: Late arrivals eat a 3,000 Balance Mana Burn, so join on time.
Starting round 2, every second round (4, 6, and so on) he strips all traps off himself, so protect key traps with Indemnity.
Indemnity protects your traps from that removal.
Every 3 rounds he casts a Shadow Meteor Strike (1,650-3,300) and puts a +200% universal Trap on himself. Hit into that self-trap for huge damage.
He may skip the self-trap if the Meteor Strike defeats a wizard.
Weakness-type spells reduce his Meteor Strike damage, so pack one.
Feinting him makes him Swap Ward the trap onto you. Use Melt or Indemnity, or skip Feint.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the swap, a safe way to trap him.
The Balance version of Feint also does not trigger it.
Every fizzle hands him 2 power pips via an Empower, so keep your accuracy high.
After his Natural Attack he puts a protected Black Mantle on you (fizzle chance), so cleanse it or lean on accuracy.
